data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260507T183602-1518e91b/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260507T183602-1518e91b/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260507T183602-1518e91b/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260507T183602-1518e91b/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260507T183602-1518e91b/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

## Decomposition depth cap
This bead is already at decomposition depth 4. Do not create another child layer; if it is still too large, reject the split with a short explanation and write no_changes_rationale.txt instead.

  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-377ebf92">
    <title>agent-modes spec: document interactive stewardship modes</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
DDx does not have a written contract that separates broad interactive queue stewardship from worker execution. An agent opened in the repo can interpret queue questions as permission to start edits, while ddx work is meant to pick up execution-ready beads.

ROOT CAUSE
AGENTS.md:1-18 enters bead policy without declaring a default interactive queue-steward mode. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:18-29 defines ddx run/try/work layers but not the interactive mode boundary around them. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-skills.md:28-32 routes phrases like "do work", "what's on the queue", and "what's ready" through the ddx skill without specifying whether those prompts mean human intervention triage or worker execution.

PROPOSED FIX
Update AGENTS.md, FEAT-010, and FEAT-011 with an explicit mode taxonomy: queue_steward for broad interactive DDx questions, bead_execution for ddx work/try/execute-bead worker prompts, direct_user_implementation for explicit human requests to edit code/docs, and review for read-only review asks. Define precedence so bead_execution supersedes only the queue-steward default, while tracker, merge-policy, and safety instructions remain load-bearing.

NON-SCOPE
- Implementing CLI behavior beyond static guidance tests.
- Changing tracker status enums or storage.
- Starting or managing workers automatically from interactive sessions.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-09d7d787. Dependencies: none.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. AGENTS.md contains a Default Interactive Mode section before Bead Policy that routes broad DDx questions to queue_steward and states that execution-ready beads belong to ddx work unless the user explicitly asks for direct implementation.
2. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md defines queue_steward, bead_execution, direct_user_implementation, and review modes, including DDX_MODE names, precedence, allowed actions, prohibited actions, and worker-capacity suggestion rules.
3. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-skills.md defines how the ddx skill routes broad interactive prompts versus explicit ddx work/try/bead execution prompts.
4. Add or update TestDDxAgentModeGuidance_DocContract to assert the guidance names all four modes and includes the ddx work carve-out.
5. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run TestDDxAgentModeGuidance_DocContract -count=1 passes.
6. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... passes.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:docs, area:specs, area:agent, kind:design, operator-ux, queue-steward, spec:FEAT-010, spec:FEAT-011</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-09d7d787" spec-id="FEAT-010,FEAT-011" base-rev="910123c01d38d703caa3c4bcc919b1aa296ea499" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260507T183602-1518e91b"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-010" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md">Feature: Task Execution</ref>
    <ref id="FEAT-011" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-skills.md">Feature: DDx Agent Skills</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
All AC satisfied. Two commits landed:

1. `87a02760d` — adds `cli/cmd/agent_mode_guidance_test.go` with `TestDDxAgentModeGuidance_DocContract`
2. `be4fdf4dc` — updates `AGENTS.md`, `FEAT-010`, and `FEAT-011` with the mode taxonomy

Every AC is verified: `Default Interactive Mode` section precedes `Bead Policy` in AGENTS.md, all four `DDX_MODE` values (`queue_steward`, `bead_execution`, `direct_user_implementation`, `review`) appear in all three documents with precedence/allowed/prohibited/worker-capacity rules, and the `ddx work` carve-out is explicit in both AGENTS.md and FEAT-011.
